summary: Large mncount decompilation pass for the Data / Records / Misc Records menu. The PR replaced much of the remaining placeholder/unmatched mncount code with typed C, semantic row/stat names, menu text creation, scrolling input handling, arrow indicator updates, and setup/init/free logic. It also renamed related gmMainLib/gm/textlib symbols and updated call sites. decomp.dev reported +1724 matched code bytes, +16 matched data bytes, 4 new matches, and many mncount functions improved into the 91-99% range.

key_files.role: Main decomp target; added typed includes, static data, row helpers, stat getters, row value selection, text row creation, input handling, arrow updates, user-data init, and menu creation.
Expanded MnCountData, added MNCOUNT_VISIBLE_ROWS, GET_MNCOUNT, mnCount_row enum, and typed prototypes for newly matched/renamed mncount functions.
Renamed many address-based symbols to semantic names for mncount, gmMainLib stat accessors, gm aggregate stat functions, and un_GetTrophyTotal.
Renamed persistent-record pointer accessors such as gmMainLib_GetVsPlayTime, gmMainLib_GetPowerCount, gmMainLib_GetTotalDamage, gmMainLib_GetKOTotal.
Renamed and updated aggregate stat helpers including gm_GetVsPlayMatchTotal, gm_GetPlayTime, and gm_IncrementPowerCount.
Updated unlock/check logic to use semantic names gm_GetVsPlayMatchTotal and un_GetTrophyTotal.
Updated Records Misc menu entry point from mnCount_8025186C to mnCount_Create.

what_changed: Replaced old local opaque fighter data access in mncount with typed persistent fields from GetPersistentFighterData, including play_time, sd_count, victories, losses, damage_dealt, and damage_taken.

Added semantic mncount getter functions for record rows: mnCount_GetMatchTime, mnCount_GetKOKingpin, mnCount_GetNoDefenseNelly, mnCount_GetDisasterMaster, mnCount_GetSmashChamp, mnCount_GetSmashSap, mnCount_GetSlugMeister, and mnCount_GetPunchingBag.

Implemented/rerouted row selection logic through mnCount_GetRowValue_Character and mnCount_GetRowValue_Number, covering rows such as POWER_COUNT, PLAY_TIME, VS_PLAY_MATCH_TOTAL, TROPHY_TOTAL, NAME_TOTAL, LONGEST_TIME, SMASH_CHAMP, and DISASTER_MASTER.
Implemented mnCount_CreateRow to create/free HSD_Text labels and values, choose time/character/number formatting, use mnCount_sis_idx text IDs, and display '-' for unavailable/tied character rows.
Implemented mnCount_HandleUserInput for Records Misc navigation: back returns to records, up/down changes scroll_pos, and rows are regenerated after scrolling.
Renamed mnCount_802513F4 to mnCount_UpdateArrowIndicators and implemented visibility of up/down arrows based on scroll_pos and MAX_SCROLL.

Renamed mnCount_802517E0 to mnCount_InitUserData and mnCount_8025186C to mnCount_Create; creation now loads MenMainConCo archive sections, creates GObjs/procs, allocates MnCountData, initializes text title, and installs input processing.
Expanded mncount.h with MnCountData.scroll_pos, expand_anim_duration, labels/values arrays, title, a GET_MNCOUNT macro, MNCOUNT_VISIBLE_ROWS, and the mnCount_row enum.

Renamed gmMainLib persistent stat accessors from address names to semantic names such as gmMainLib_GetVsPlayContestants, gmMainLib_GetVsPlayTime, gmMainLib_GetTimeMatchTotal, gmMainLib_GetStockMatchTotal, gmMainLib_GetCoinMatchTotal, gmMainLib_GetBonusMatchTotal, gmMainLib_GetSingleplayerTime, gmMainLib_GetPowerCount, gmMainLib_GetTotalDamage, gmMainLib_GetKOTotal, and gmMainLib_GetSelfDestructTotal.

Renamed gm_8016279C to gm_GetVsPlayMatchTotal, gm_80162A00 to gm_GetPlayTime, gm_80162B98 to gm_IncrementPowerCount, and un_80304870 to un_GetTrophyTotal, with call sites updated across gm, soundtest, textlib, lbcardgame, mnmain, trophy display/list code, and symbols.txt.

decomp.dev reported new full matches for mnCount_HandleUserInput, mnCount_8025072C, mncount .bss, and mnCount_8025035C; it also reported near-matches for mnCount_8025092C, mnCount_CreateRow, mnCount_GetRowValue_Number, fn_80251640, mnCount_Create, mnCount_GetRowValue_Character, and fn_802514D8.
smart_moves: The PR used semantic stat accessor names before wiring mncount row logic, making the record menu code read as game-stat logic instead of raw x1Axx field offsets.
Replacing LocalFighterData padding/unk fields with persistent fighter data field names made character-record rows much easier to audit and likely helped match small getter functions cleanly.
The mnCount_row enum documents the exact row order and labels for the Misc Records screen, which ties together SIS text IDs, scroll limits, and value computation.
Splitting value computation into character rows, time rows, and numeric rows kept mnCount_CreateRow simple enough to match while making the menu behavior understandable.
The implementation preserved match-sensitive structure with explicit inline helpers, noinline wrapper shims, and stack padding where needed instead of only pursuing source readability.
Updating symbols.txt and all dependent call sites in the same change avoided semantic names diverging from linker symbols.
decomp_lessons: For records/stat menus, name the underlying persistent-stat accessors first; semantic accessors reveal what each menu row is supposed to display.
Typed save/persistent-data structs are high leverage: replacing manual padded local structs with real fields can unlock both matching and reviewability.
A row enum with comments is useful when UI code indexes labels, scroll positions, and switch statements by the same ordinal values.
When decompiling menu pages, model the full lifecycle: archive load, GObj creation, user-data allocation/init, proc installation, text creation/freeing, input polling, and exit cleanup.
Keep visible-row count and max-scroll constants explicit; here 30 total mnCount_row entries, 10 visible rows, and MAX_SCROLL 20 describe the Records Misc list behavior.
If a helper must inline or must not inline to match, capture that in source structure with static inline helpers or wrapper functions rather than hiding the tactic.
When evidence is weak for a function's purpose, leaving names like fn_802514D8 and fn_80251640 is safer than inventing semantics; this PR only renamed functions with clearer evidence.
naming_conventions: mncount semantic functions use the module prefix plus Pascal-style verb/object names, e.g. mnCount_GetRowValue_Number, mnCount_CreateRow, mnCount_HandleUserInput, mnCount_UpdateArrowIndicators, mnCount_InitUserData, and mnCount_Create.
Record row enum constants use all-caps menu/stat labels such as POWER_COUNT, VS_PLAY_MATCH_TOTAL, SELFDESTRUCT_TOTAL, AVAILABLE_CHARACTERS, KO_KINGPIN, NO_DEFENSE_NELLY, and DISASTER_MASTER.
gmMainLib_Get* names identify pointer accessors into persistent global record data, e.g. gmMainLib_GetVsPlayTime and gmMainLib_GetKOTotal.
gm_Get* names identify higher-level aggregate values such as gm_GetVsPlayMatchTotal and gm_GetPlayTime; gm_IncrementPowerCount names a mutating helper.
un_GetTrophyTotal replaced the raw address name un_80304870 and was propagated to textlib, trophy display/list, and unlock-check call sites.
Address comments in headers were retained while prototypes were renamed, preserving decomp navigation between addresses and semantic symbols.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: mnCount_8025035C kept PAD_STACK(4), indicating stack layout remained match-sensitive even after semantic cleanup.
mnCount_UpdateArrowIndicators contains an explicit note that GET_JOBJ breaks the match, so it uses gobj->hsd_obj directly.
Static inline helpers were used for row classification, row refresh, text freeing, title creation, and zero-play-time checks, matching likely inlined code shapes.
Noinline wrapper functions such as mnCount_UpdateArrowIndicators_noinline and mnCount_InitUserData_noinline were introduced to force call boundaries where needed.
The code uses small macros GET_KOS and GET_FALLS around tie-breaker logic in mnCount_8025072C, keeping repeated getter expressions consistent.
Static globals such as model_desc, menu_gobj, menu_jobj, and mnCount_sis_idx contributed to data/BSS progress; the bot reported mncount .bss became 100% matched.
review_feedback: No human PR body, reviews, or inline review comments were present in the slice.
decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code increased to 60.62% with +0.04% / +1724 bytes and matched data increased by +16 bytes.
decomp.dev bot listed 4 new full matches: mnCount_HandleUserInput, mnCount_8025072C, mncount .bss, and mnCount_8025035C.

decomp.dev bot listed 10 improved but not fully matched items, including mnCount_8025092C at 99.35%, mnCount_CreateRow at 96.51%, mnCount_GetRowValue_Number at 95.47%, fn_80251640 at 96.30%, mnCount_Create at 91.24%, mnCount_GetRowValue_Character at 99.33%, and fn_802514D8 at 96.27%.
follow_up_queries: mnCount_8025092C remaining mismatch 99.35 tie breaker sort ranking
mnCount_CreateRow remaining mismatch HSD_SisLib text formatting time rows
fn_802514D8 fn_80251640 mncount remaining menu lifecycle matches
gmMainLib_8015CD5C remaining unnamed persistent record stat
